concept: Role / Defined Position in a Group or Institution
description: A defined position within an institution or a social arrangement, kept separate from whoever currently holds it. A role is defined well when someone can say who selects its holder, what it permits, and how it passes to someone else.
sound_symbolism: Fine 'si' begins with a narrow hiss and focused vowel, one place marked inside a larger sound. Stressed 'tho' breathes along the teeth and rounds the center. 'Ra' rolls outward into broad 'a'. The role starts as a small position, gains a voiced shape, and opens into the activity around it.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'lo mia ha sithora kirothe.' (We define this role.) State holder, selection, functions, permissions, obligations, term, and handoff where needed. Distinct from 'kelitho' (technical function), 'porua' (purpose), 'thonai' (responsible), profession, and an exact office title.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Explicit roles make maintenance, coordination, and care visible while allowing rotation, shared responsibility, and institutional redesign.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Separating a role from its holder prevents office, expertise, status, or assigned duty from becoming a claim about the person's inherent value or authority.

semantic_domains.activity: a defined position associated with stated functions and obligations
semantic_domains.community: a social or institutional role with bounded scope
